data.four_set_effect: Increases DMG against opponents affected by Pyro by 35%.

circlet_of_logos.desc: The circlet of a sage who traversed a sea of fire. It once shone brightly from their ancient silhouette as they stood strong amidst the flames.
circlet_of_logos.title: Lavawalker's Wisdom

flower_of_life.desc: A flower that blooms amidst burning flames. It is said that long ago, a sage once wore it as he walked into a sea of fire.
flower_of_life.title: Lavawalker's Resolution

goblet_of_eonothem.desc: A legendary goblet that can withstand extremely high temperatures. It still retains a degree of warmth even though it is now empty.
goblet_of_eonothem.title: Lavawalker's Epiphany

plume_of_death.desc: The feather of a proud phoenix. You can almost hear the sound of its wings flapping in the scorching flames.
plume_of_death.title: Lavawalker's Salvation

sands_of_eon.desc: Burning sand flows within this hourglass. Despite the intense heat, the sand leave no mark upon the vessel that houses it.
sands_of_eon.title: Lavawalker's Torment

list.desc: A flower that only blooms in the burning flames.</br>The searing pain it causes will make the wearer stronger.</br>The fiery-red flower shines like shimmering agate.</br>This flaming flower was once a brooch worn by the Lavawalker.</br>In the Lavawalker's final days, he said to his people:</br>"This is the flower that blooms in flames. Until the day that the fires turn me into ashes,</br>You shall see the glowing embers of this flower shining through the dark smoke."</br>The people followed the flower's flickering light to the edge of the Mare Jivari.</br>There they found the blooming flowers — but the Lavawalker was nowhere to be seen.</br>
list.title: Lavawalker's Resolution

list.desc: An ancient circlet that once belonged to the Lavawalker — a sage who wandered in the Mare Jivari.</br>Upon close examination, one can almost see his figure standing strong amidst the fiery flames.</br>The wandering sage of the Mare Jivari known as the Lavawalker crafted this circlet from crimson agate to resist the intense heat of the flames.</br>The circlet was built with wisdom and tenacity. It sparked fear and jealousy among peers and seniors alike.</br>"How dare the Lavawalker challenge the flames of the Mare Jivari! Such blasphemy has not been seen in hundreds of years!</br>The flaming sea will surely devour this arrogant young man and the sky will blow his ashes into the void."</br>In his jealousy, the teacher demanded that his student step into the flaming sea wearing the circlet.</br>In a state of calmness, the Lavawalker strolled onto the lava and gradually disappeared into the horizon.</br>
list.title: Lavawalker's Wisdom

list.desc: A goblet that once contained lava. Now empty, it still has a slight hint of warmth.</br>This goblet once belonged to a sage known as the Lavawalker. Wisdom flowed from within.</br>The Lavawalker would toy with the goblet. Even in the blazing heat, it remained intact.</br>Legend has it that the Lavawalker would drink lava from this goblet as if it were a glass of fine wine.</br>But while fine wine evaporates in high heat, the wisdom of the sage could withstand a trial by fire.</br>To the Lavawalker, fine wine was merely a catalyst in the process of producing ingenuity.</br>Aided by alcohol, even the slightest spark can combust into a brilliant idea.</br>The goblet was a silent witness to the forging of wisdom amidst the flames.</br>The moment before the Lavawalker set off on his final journey was when the goblet was filled with the most pride.</br>
list.title: Lavawalker's Epiphany

list.desc: A feather plucked by the Lavawalker from a bird that sings amid the flames.</br>The wearer can almost hear the sound of the bird's wings flapping in the blaze.</br>Legend has it that there exists a kind of solitary bird that sings in the blazing flames.</br>People worshiped it as a totem while kings saw it as a sign of nobility.</br>Donning this feather, the Lavawalker retreated from the outside world.</br>Alone he came and alone he went, leaving no trace behind.</br>Since then, people often hear cries coming from the Mare Jivari.</br>No one knows if they are the song of the bird or the sighs of the Lavawalker.</br>
list.title: Lavawalker's Salvation

list.desc: What flows within this hourglass is not regular sand, but burning sand.</br>Like this burning sand, time flows forward, leaving no trace of its own existence.</br>The story of the Lavawalker continued even after he had traveled across the Mare Jivari.</br>It is said that he spent another one hundred years living as a hermit.</br>But this short retreat brought little relief from his burning torment.</br>During his time as a secluded sage, the Lavawalker could not bear the eternal scorch. He crafted this hourglass to mark the passing of time.</br>As the flames blazed, burning sand flowed endlessly through the hourglass day after day.</br>Sadly, though the Lavawalker did not fear the scorching flames of the Mare Jivari, he could not quench the torturing flames of time.</br>The anguish of being left alone without the disciples and family he held so dear burned away at his soul more than any flames could ever scorch his flesh.</br>
list.title: Lavawalker's Torment
